summary: Phase 13 — Public Atlas (SEO + Lead Capture) end-to-end testing complete. All 13 active backend pytest tests pass (1 skipped by design). All 14 frontend Playwright checks pass: hub page (/atlas), country list (/atlas/au), single occupation (/atlas/au/261313) — all render WITHOUT auth in headless browser. SEO meta tags + JSON-LD (@type=Occupation) + canonical URLs injected correctly. Lead form submission end-to-end works and lead-success state appears. Honeypot drops, invalid email returns 422, draft codes not exposed, country search filters correctly.

minor.issue: Rate limit (15 req/60s/IP) does NOT engage in the preview environment when RUN_RATELIMIT_TEST=1 — 20 consecutive requests all returned 200. Likely cause: request.client.host returns the K8s ingress proxy address differently per request (or the in-memory _ip_log cleared due to multiple uvicorn workers / hot-reload). Recommend using X-Forwarded-For (first hop) and/or moving state into MongoDB so rate-limit survives across workers.

issues: og:image is a RELATIVE path '/og-atlas.png' — Facebook/LinkedIn/Twitter previews require absolute URL (e.g., https://<host>/og-atlas.png). Same for link[rel=canonical] which is '/atlas/au/261313' instead of absolute.

Occupation page <title> reads 'Software Engineer (261313) — Australia Legacy migration · 2026-05-22 Migration Guide | LEAMSS' — the 'Legacy migration · 2026-05-22' fragment looks like a concatenation glitch; recommend cleaning to just '— Australia Migration Guide | LEAMSS'.
Featured grid rendered 11 occupation cards instead of expected 12 (one short of the spec).
No JSON-LD script tag injected on hub page — only the single-occupation page injects it. If hub SEO is desired (e.g., ItemList schema), add it.

action_items: [MINOR] Make og:image and canonical absolute URLs (prefix with REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL or window.location.origin).
[MINOR] Sanitize occupation page <title> — remove the date/'Legacy migration' fragment.
[MINOR] Featured grid returns 11 items, spec says 12 — verify /api/public-atlas/featured response length or frontend slicing.
[OPTIONAL] Move rate-limit state to MongoDB or use X-Forwarded-For so the 429 enforcement works behind the ingress.
critical_code_review_comments: public_atlas.py uses in-memory _ip_log dict — non-persistent + non-shared across workers. Won't survive a backend restart and won't work in multi-replica deploy. For an unauthenticated public endpoint this is the most likely abuse vector; should be hardened.
PublicAtlas.jsx applySEO() injects meta tags via direct DOM manipulation. Works, but consider react-helmet-async for SSR-safety if the app moves to SSG/SSR later for SEO.
Honeypot returns 200 + lead_id='honeypot_dropped' — good UX (bot doesn't know it was dropped). Verified working.
test_credentials: admin@leamss.com / Admin@123 (only used to verify lead persistence via /api/marketing/leads). All public atlas endpoints work WITHOUT auth as required.
seed_data_creation: Created two real leads via API + one via UI (emails: qa+<ts>@test.example.com, qa+stored+<ts>@test.example.com, qa+ui+<ts>@test.example.com). Stored in 'leads' collection with source='public_atlas'. Honeypot lead was correctly NOT persisted.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase 13 fully validated. Pytest suite at /app/backend/tests/test_phase13_public_atlas.py — 13 pass + 1 skipped (rate-limit; set RUN_RATELIMIT_TEST=1 to run, currently FAILS in preview env behind ingress proxy — known limitation). Frontend tests must wait for /featured API response (~2s) before asserting on country cards or featured grid. Use data-testid='atlas-hub-country-{AU|CA|NZ}' (uppercase), 'atlas-country-search', 'atlas-country-grid', 'atlas-occ-root', 'atlas-lead-form', 'atlas-lead-{name|email|phone|message|submit}', 'atlas-lead-success', 'atlas-country-error'. Hub page does NOT inject JSON-LD; only single occupation page does (@type='Occupation').
rca of the issue: Rate-limit non-engagement RCA: _rate_limit() keys off request.client.host. Behind Kubernetes ingress with multiple uvicorn workers (or session-bucketed connections), each worker has an independent _ip_log dict, AND request.client.host may resolve to the immediate L4 hop (different per connection). Fix: (1) read request.headers.get('x-forwarded-for','').split(',')[0].strip() as the real client IP, AND (2) move the deque/counter into MongoDB with TTL so it's worker-shared and persistent.
